title: QUIC Connection Runtime High-Density Execution Architecture
purpose: Describe the endpoint-side sharded runtime host that routes a stable connection handle to one owning shard and lets each shard process many connection runtimes without a thread per connection.
design_summary:
The high-density profile keeps `QuicConnectionRuntime` as the single-owner runtime object, but the endpoint owns a bounded set of shard loops and a concurrent route registry.
A stable internal handle selects one shard for the lifetime of the connection, and every event for that handle is routed through the same shard inbox.
The shard loop owns many runtimes, processes events serially for each shard, and keeps concurrent collections at endpoint scope rather than inside connection-owned state.
Timer migration, endpoint-wide scheduling, and other downstream CRT slices remain separate concerns.
risks: Route-table churn must update ownership explicitly so handle-to-shard affinity never shifts after registration.
A runtime that enters the dedicated-owner profile must not also be registered to a shard host.
Timer ownership is still a follow-up slice and should not be inferred from this architecture.

supplemental_sections.heading: Routing Model
supplemental_sections.content:
A `QuicConnectionHandle` is the stable ownership key.
Shard selection is deterministic and pure, so repeated lookups for the same handle return the same shard index for the lifetime of the host.

supplemental_sections.heading: Shard Ownership
supplemental_sections.content:
Each shard owns a single inbox and consumes work items for many runtimes.
The shard does not share mutable connection state across consumers, and routing lookups happen at endpoint scope before the shard accepts work.
